graded_findings.rationale: This user-facing logging guide still describes the removed log_rate_limit model and backward compatibility that no longer holds. That is a material documentation mismatch for changed operational configuration.

The canonical logging design documentation still centers the removed log_rate_limit and LogRateLimiter behavior. For an infrastructure logging change, stale canonical design docs can mislead future maintenance and review.
Trace context sampling semantics changed, but this doc still describes decisions through the old local log_rate_limit path. That is a substantive mismatch in shared tracing behavior.
Access logging behavior now includes access_sample_rate pre-drop and facade semantics, but the module doc was not updated. This is a real adherence gap for an operationally relevant logging path.

The PLOG sampling behavior changed from the documented bypass design to diagnostic_sample_rate/request sampled-in handling. Leaving the plan with the old design is a substantive inconsistency for diagnostic logging behavior.
